Aham.bio: Aham (born Cristian Navarro Salgado) is a Barcelona-born contact improvisation and conscious movement facilitator. Before finding CI, he trained extensively in martial arts, acrobatics, parkour, dance, gymnastics, and acting, and discovered contact improvisation through physical theatre work. He now travels internationally teaching workshops that blend embodiment, movement, and connection practices, and is a repeat presenter at Ängsbacka's festivals.

Anand Rudra.bio: Anand Rudra is a tantra, shamanism, and breathwork facilitator who teaches under the banner of ancestral and ceremonial technologies for self-realization. He runs the school/brand TrueTantra and offers courses such as Tantra Kriya Yoga, blending tantric yoga, ceremony, and energy practices. He has taught internationally and is a recurring presenter at Ängsbacka's Tantra Festival, where his workshops focus on sacred touch, presence, and ritual/ceremony work.
Anand Rudra.location: Europe (exact home base unclear; associated with TrueTantra, an internationally touring school)

Anders Lorentz.bio: Anders Lorentz is a Danish tantra and breathwork facilitator based in Copenhagen, founder of Lifeforce Garden and co-founder of the Copenhagen Tantra Festival, Denmark's largest tantra event. With over a decade of experience, he teaches embodiment, conscious sexuality, and energy work through breath, body, and movement, often working with couples seeking deeper presence and vitality. He is a certified Advanced SOMA Breath facilitator and trained in Prana Technology under tantra teacher Anand Rudra, and frequently co-teaches with his partner Kate Aura.

Andy Buru.bio: Andy Buru is a Stockholm/Berlin-based facilitator whose work centers on 'the intimacy of power' — exploring conscious and consensual power dynamics through modalities including sadomasochism, esoteric eroticism, Japanese rope bondage (shibari), and neuro-somatic bodywork. Their background spans European theatre studies, medical massage therapy, and study of Japanese arts including sado (tea ceremony), kado (flower arranging), and aikido. They also work as a writer, retreat curator, ritual crafter, and photographer, taking select one-on-one clients.

Antonia Rothschild.bio: Antonia Frida (formerly known publicly as Antonia Rothschild) is a multidisciplinary artist, dancer, and embodiment facilitator who describes her guiding philosophy as 'truth as her compass, love as the map.' She works across writing, dance, singing, healing, and visual art, drawing on roughly 8 years of experience and formal training in yoga and movement. She has published written work (featured on Elephant Journal) and recently released a debut music album, and now teaches embodiment and polarity/passion-themed workshops such as those at Ängsbacka's festivals.
Antonia Rothschild.location: Not clearly stated; appears to be based in Europe

Board member Paul Hurcomb.bio: Paul Hurcomb is a UK-based (Eastbourne area) spiritual teacher and former schoolteacher who spent twelve years as a practicing Buddhist before a profound awakening into presence in 2002. He studied with Sri Mooji and works within the self-inquiry tradition associated with Ramana Maharshi, holding retreats and meetings (Meetings in Stillness) both online and in person since 2010. He has a long-standing connection to Ängsbacka as a recurring teacher and community figure, and authored the book 'The Union of Love and Wisdom' (2023).
Board member Paul Hurcomb.location: Eastbourne, United Kingdom (also associated with Ängsbacka, Sweden)

Buzz.bio: Buzz Lambert is a Tantra facilitator with nearly three decades of experience, blending traditional tantric practice with modern relational tools such as nonviolent communication (NVC) and therapeutic touch. He runs Tantric Bodywork and Embodiment sessions and is a recurring teacher at Ängsbacka, where his work centers on conscious communication and embodied intimacy.

Concert Tobias.bio: Tobias Lundgren, performing under the name T.Léon, is a musician and former commercial songwriter/producer who has worked with major artists including Céline Dion and Chris Stapleton. He has since moved into ceremonial and transformational music, creating frequency-based songs, chants, and guided musical journeys drawn from inner exploration and ancestral memory. He currently collaborates with Swedish joik artist Jon Henrik Fjällgren and with cellist Hale Heikkilä.

Conor Foley.bio: Conor Foley is a Shibari-focused facilitator who has been exploring tantra and rope bondage since around 2022, blending rope work, breath, presence, and somatic awareness into consent-based experiences. Trained as a tantra therapist through The Art of Love, his approach treats rope as a tool for physical dialogue and emotional exploration rather than pure aesthetics, emphasizing trust and embodied connection between participants.

David Ol-Lars.bio: David Ol-Lars is a Stockholm-based facilitator and the CEO of Practise, a Swedish firm specializing in leadership and team development. Since 2014 he has combined a background in team/leadership development within special police units with training in Somatic Experiencing, tantra therapy, Embodied Intimacy, and shadow work, and he also provides crisis support. His workshops focus on creating safety and presence so participants can move beyond their comfort zones.

Gabriella Eriksson.display_name: Gabriella Eriksson (Earth & Heart Sisters, with Cecilia)
Gabriella Eriksson.bio: Gabriella Eriksson co-founded Earth & Heart Sisters together with Cecilia, a Kungsbacka, Sweden-based collective offering sacred feminine embodiment work, cacao ceremonies, breathwork, and sound healing. She works with Sensing Yoga, tantra therapy, kundalini activation, Reiki and holistic coaching, guiding women to reconnect with their bodies and inner wisdom. At Ängsbacka she facilitates 'Sacred Feminine Movements,' a flowing, intuitive movement practice meant to help participants embody feminine essence and sensuality.

Guro von Germeten.bio: Guro von Germeten is a Norwegian artist, voice researcher, and Kundalini yoga teacher based in Nesodden, Norway, where she runs her own studio and temple space, Azure Vault. She holds a PhD from the Norwegian Academy of Music on vocal technique, and works professionally as a singer and complete vocal technique teacher. She is also training as a Love, Sex, and Relationship Coach through Layla Martin's VITA program. At Ängsbacka she leads a series of themed early-morning Kundalini Yoga and ceremony sessions designed to awaken vitality and align body, soul, and sensual power.

Hale.bio: Hale Heikkilä is a Finnish multi-instrumentalist and vocalist based in Fiskars, Finland, playing cello, keyboards, rhythm instruments and voice. Classically trained but drawn to improvisation and intuitive, electronic-tinged composition, his music is inspired by nature and Lapland's landscapes. He performs solo sound-medicine sessions and also collaborates with other artists, e.g. as a duo with Tobias Lundgren/T. Léon. At Ängsbacka he offers jam sessions and 'music from within' style ecstatic/sound-healing performances.

Hale Heikkilä.bio: Hale Heikkilä is a Finnish multi-instrumentalist and vocalist based in Fiskars, Finland, playing cello, keyboards, rhythm instruments and voice. Classically trained but drawn to improvisation and intuitive, electronic-tinged composition, his music is inspired by nature and Lapland's landscapes. He performs solo sound-medicine sessions and also collaborates with other artists, e.g. as a duo with Tobias Lundgren/T. Léon, and on the immersive breathwork project 'Symphony of Breath' with Leigh Ewin. At Ängsbacka his 'SoundMedicine' session uses sound and music as a healing, meditative practice.

Holy Tree.bio: Holy Tree is a musical duo/collective centered at Ängsbacka, creating shamanic, improvisational 'musical journeys' built on flute, voice, and heart-centered presence. Founded by Filip Fišer, described as a 'music medicine man' and also founder of Ängsbacka's Sound Festival, alongside singer-songwriter and vocal coach Malin Wik. The project is currently working on a debut album titled 'River of Life.'

Ilja Samsonov.bio: Ilja Samsonov is a tantra therapist and workshop facilitator with over fifteen years of experience leading groups and individuals in embodied, relational work. His approach is grounded, poetic, and playful, focused on presence, play, and transformation. He offers tantra massage, tantra therapy, and a 6-month immersive journey program called 'Seen, Held and Heard.' At Ängsbacka he facilitates sessions exploring relating dynamics ('Me, You and Them') and creative/poetic expression within a tantric frame ('Tantra Poetry').

Ingela Axcrantz Steele.bio: Ingela is a certified Yin and Hatha yoga teacher and a registered nurse who spent over 20 years working with dying people. She runs her own retreat center, The Crystal Barn, near Ängsbacka in Molkom, where she leads Yin Yoga workshops, darkness retreats, and sound journeys using gongs and crystal bowls. She is also a published writer and poet, author of the book 'Candles in Darkness'. Her work centers on creating safe spaces for people to relax into love and true presence.

Jack Sylla.bio: Jack Sylla is a Guinean percussionist and dance teacher known in his homeland as 'Le génie de Guinée' (the genius of Guinea), having worked alongside major figures in traditional and popular African music. Since 2000 he has taught djembe percussion and West African dance throughout Sweden. He is the lead/soloist drummer for the Guinea-based music and dance troupe Les Étoiles de Boulbinet Guinée, and is regarded as a master djembe teacher. He is based in the Stockholm area, where he also gives private drum lessons.

Janie Petersen¥.bio: Janie is a tantra teacher, love and relationship coach, and creative artist who studied with renowned tantra teacher Layla Martin. She lives in Sweden with her husband Fredrik Swahn, with whom she runs a 'Romantic Nature Resort' retreat center in Stjärnsund and co-facilitates couples retreats and tantra events internationally. Art and creativity have played a central role in her personal journey of embodying sensuality and sexuality, which she now channels into workshops blending drawing/art practices with erotic and tantric embodiment.

Jessica Magnusson.bio: Jessica Magnusson is the founder of Roots by Love, a Sweden-based practice guiding people back to their true essence through tantric wisdom and bodily awareness. She works as a tantra therapist specializing in somatic consent, blending intuitive dialogue with conscious touch to help clients safely explore boundaries and desires. Her approach emphasizes embodied spirituality, helping participants land more fully in their bodies, meet life with an open heart, and embrace vulnerability while finding their own grounded roots.

Julia.bio: Julia Akacia is a German-born tantra teacher and coach initiated into the Sri Vidya lineage of classical Tantra, certified in Tantra Yoga and Ashtanga Yoga (750+ training hours) and trained as a Rebirthing Breathwork facilitator. In 2015 she received initiation (deeksha) from Sri Amritananda of Devipuram in Andhra Pradesh, India, and her signature offering is the Kalavahana ritual, a Sri Vidya ceremony that ritually worships the recipient's body as an embodiment of the Goddess. She has lived and taught for over 15 years across Asia, Africa, Australia and the Americas, and now bases herself in Guatemala and Mexico.
Julia.location: Based in Guatemala/Mexico; born in Germany

Julia Akacia.bio: Julia Akacia is a German-born tantra teacher and coach initiated into the Sri Vidya lineage of classical Tantra, certified in Tantra Yoga and Ashtanga Yoga (750+ training hours) and trained as a Rebirthing Breathwork facilitator. In 2015 she received initiation (deeksha) from Sri Amritananda of Devipuram in Andhra Pradesh, India. She has lived and taught for over 15 years across Asia, Africa, Australia and the Americas, and now bases herself in Guatemala and Mexico, teaching Tantra Yoga, somatic nervous-system healing and breathwork internationally and online.
Julia Akacia.location: Based in Guatemala/Mexico; born in Germany

Katarina Nilsson.bio: Katarina Nilsson is a tantra therapist and embodiment coach with more than 15 years of experience in energy work, devoted to creating spaces where the body can soften and inner truth can expand. Her practice integrates tantra therapy, shamanic breathwork, conscious touch and tantric bodywork, and she teaches on themes of boundaries, embodiment and touch. She runs her practice under the name Heartment.

Kate Aura.bio: Kate Aura (Kate Burkal) is a Danish tantra facilitator and transformation guide based in Copenhagen, with more than 20 years of experience in teaching and mental health work and nearly a decade immersed in tantra. She co-founded several of Denmark's largest tantra and conscious-love events, including the Copenhagen Tantra Festival, Kærlig Festival and Vital Festival. Her workshops focus on embodied practice, sacred sexuality, desire and energy work, and she frequently co-facilitates couples-oriented sessions with her partner Anders Lorentz.

Lexmy.bio: Lexmy van den Boogaard is a Dutch tantra facilitator, coach and community creator based in Amsterdam who also teaches across Europe, Mexico and Thailand. She walks a Tantric path rooted in devotional spiritual practice blended with a background in science and corporate business, and is known for creating temple-style ceremonial spaces and collective 'fields'. Her work uses love and sexuality as pathways to liberation and self-realization, and she co-organizes tantra festivals internationally.

Lexmy van den Boogaard.bio: Lexmy van den Boogaard is a Dutch tantra facilitator, coach and community creator based in Amsterdam who also teaches across Europe, Mexico and Thailand. Her offerings include 'Energetic Lovemaking' and group ritual/field-transmission work, as well as the 'R.B.D.S.M.A.' talk — a consent-and-communication framework (Relationship, Boundaries, Desires, Sexual health, Meaning, Aftercare) used widely in tantra and conscious-relating circles.

Lin.bio: Lin Holmquist is one of Europe's foremost tantra therapists and teachers, with over 15 years of experience as a therapist (the last decade centered specifically on tantra). Her path began in the primordial shamanism of the north, continued with study of classical yoga and tantra in India, and later moved into neotantra; a powerful kundalini awakening in 2011 became the turning point that led her to devote herself fully to guiding others through their own awakening processes. She is the founder of The Art of Love school of tantra and has trained over 100 tantra therapists worldwide, teaching that pleasure can be used as a healing method. She is also a yoga therapist, meditation teacher, NLP practitioner, doula, author, and dakini.

Lisanne Savitri.bio: Lisanne Savitri is a Sauna Master trained in Germany (Aufguss sauna rituals), a yoga teacher, space holder, and massage therapist based in Värmland, Sweden. Her practice emphasizes presence, deep listening, and creating safe, nurturing spaces for relaxation and reconnection. She offers Lomi Lomi Nordic-style massage, deep-tissue and relaxation full-body massage, and sauna/Aufguss rituals, describing her approach as 'a gentle invitation to slow down, soften, and come back home to the body.'

Loló.display_name: Loló Gómez Ortega (probable; paired with Tobias Lundgren)
Loló.bio: Loló Gómez Ortega carries dance and music in her blood, born into a lineage of professional dancers, musicians and healers. She has spent over 20 years designing transformational gatherings worldwide, trained in Zen Coaching, Family Constellation & Systemic Movements, Bodywork & Massage Therapy, Chinese Reflexology, Reiki, Breathwork, Meditation, Yoga, Qi Gong, Pilates, and Bioenergetic Movement & Contact Improvisation. She is credited as creator of 'Hands from Above' and 'Body – Gateway to Heart and Spirit,' and co-facilitates the live music/touch journey 'Held in Sound' at Ängsbacka's Tantra Festival together with musician Tobias Lundgren (T.Léon).
Loló.location: Unconfirmed; multi-decade international career suggests Spanish/Latin heritage

Luna Zen Kitty.display_name: Seung Yeon Oh (stage name Luna Zen Kitty)
Luna Zen Kitty.bio: Luna Zen Kitty (Seung Yeon Oh) is a tea ritualist and DJ who creates immersive ceremonial spaces. Raised by a mother who studied tea ceremony culture, she trained in Korean Royal Tea Ritual and is deepening her practice in Japanese Senchado. She has reportedly served tea for the Queen of Bhutan at an Innovation Forum. As a DJ, her sets blend deep, dark, sensual bass layered with mystical melodies and textures for sacred/ritual and temple-style spaces.
Luna Zen Kitty.location: Not stated beyond her association with Ängsbacka; possible Korean heritage/background

Michael Finkel.bio: Michael Finkel is an embodiment facilitator, Tantra teacher, and Nonviolent Communication (NVC) trainer with 15+ years of experience in conscious sexuality, intimacy, and personal growth. He is also a professional aerial circus artist and organizes Tel Aviv's Open Air Tango Milonga. His work blends Embodiment, Tantra, Conscious Touch, Conscious Relating, Radical Honesty, and NVC to create transformational spaces of deep intimacy and honesty. He is artistic director for some of Israel's leading Tantra events, a father of two, and teaches retreats/workshops/festivals across Israel and Europe.

Nadine Edstrand.bio: Nadine Edstrand is a Stockholm-based facilitator specializing in Authentic Relating, Ecstatic Dance, and Yin Yoga. She co-founded Ecstatic Dance Journey Stockholm, The Journey Festival, and Ecstatica Sweden, building communities around conscious movement and connection. She describes herself as a yoga instructor, authentic relating facilitator, and coach who is passionate about community, love, and inclusion. Her workshops help people find comfort in their own bodies among others and discover authentic ways of moving and expressing themselves through movement and music.

Nina Möller.bio: Nina Möller is the owner of Inre Natur, a community space in Örebro, Sweden dedicated to personal growth and holistic wellbeing. Coming from a social work background, she combines warmth, presence, and softness in her facilitation style. She leads workshops exploring presence, intimacy, boundaries, and communication, with her flagship offering being Conscious Closeness (Närvarande Närhet), which uses partner and group exercises to explore physical and emotional connection, consent, and touch.

Philip Flow.bio: Philip Flow (Philip Sigfridsson) is a Neurosomatics & Breathwork facilitator, yoga teacher, and international educator with over a decade of teaching experience. His work blends modern neuroscience, nervous system regulation, somatic awareness, and evidence-informed breathwork practices to help people reduce stress, build resilience, and reconnect with their capacity for healing and performance. Workshop titles like 'Breath of Becoming' and 'Neurodive' reflect his focus on breathwork and nervous-system-oriented somatic education.

Punnu Wasu.bio: Punnu Wasu (Punnu Singh Wasu) is an internationally recognized kirtan singer, multi-instrumentalist, meditation teacher, and Reiki grandmaster from India. Born into a family devoted to kirtan and spirituality, he began learning kirtan at age five and trained in Hindustani classical music from early childhood. He now splits his time between Bali and Switzerland, leading meditation and kirtan trainings, teacher certifications, and sound-healing programs worldwide, including regular appearances at The Yoga Barn in Bali.
Punnu Wasu.location: Based in Ubud, Bali, Indonesia and Switzerland; originally from India

Renzo Aneröd.bio: Renzo Aneröd has a varied physical background as a wrestler, Thai boxer, and bodybuilder who competed and coached in these disciplines before moving into bodywork. He trained in Swedish classical massage, Thai massage, tantra massage, trigger point massage, de-armouring, and chakra massage, and has taught various meditation and mindfulness methods. He developed 'Conscious Workout & Bodywork,' a method combining physical activity, body presence, meditation, and breathwork to cultivate presence across body, mind, and soul. Beyond his bodywork teaching, he is also a writer, filmmaker, director, and musician.
Renzo Aneröd.location: Sweden (closely associated with Ängsbacka Kursgård in Värmland)

Richards Azens.bio: Rihards Azens is a Latvian pirts (ceremonial steam bathing) master with over a decade of experience working with the body through touch, heat, breath, and presence. He co-founded Forestum, a wellness retreat near Ikšķile outside Riga, Latvia, where he leads traditional Latvian pirts rituals, therapeutic bodywork, and forest bathing experiences. His approach blends the ancient pirts tradition with tantra-informed principles of vulnerability and authentic connection.

Rihards Azens.bio: Rihards Azens is a Latvian pirts (ceremonial steam bathing) master with over a decade of experience working with the body through touch, heat, breath, and presence. He co-founded Forestum, a wellness retreat near Ikšķile outside Riga, Latvia, where he leads traditional Latvian pirts rituals, therapeutic bodywork, and forest bathing experiences. At Ängsbacka he leads the multi-day Latvian Pirts Ritual sessions during the Tantra Festival.

Simon Paul Sutton.bio: Simon Paul Sutton is an international facilitator, speaker, sex educator, and tantric practitioner with over two decades of experience in training and facilitation. A certified Sexological Bodyworker, former professional actor, personal trainer, and sports massage therapist, he blends somatic practice, presence, and playful yet compassionate space-holding. He is the founder of NAKED Soul and Soul Pleasure, and authored the book 'Burglar to Buddha: Transforming from the inside out.'

Teresa Ribu.bio: Teresa Ribu is a facilitator specializing in women's sensual and sexual embodiment, drawing on pole dance, somatic bodywork, meditation, yoga, and movement practices. She guides participants toward embodied presence, self-love, and feminine power, helping people release sexual shame and guilt while reconnecting with the body, pleasure, and feminine energy. She founded Salty Pines Studio and created the 'Healing with Heels' concept.

The Art of Love's Therapists.display_name: The Art of Love (tantra therapy collective, founded by Lin Holmquist)
The Art of Love's Therapists.bio: The Art of Love is a Swedish tantra therapy school and collective founded by therapist Lin Holmquist, who has worked in therapy for 15+ years (the last decade focused on tantra) and has trained over 100 certified tantra therapists worldwide through her 200-hour Tantra Therapist Training. At Ängsbacka's Tantra Festival, graduates of this training staff rotating 'temple' spaces offering touch, cuddle, and emotional-release sessions to festival participants. Lin Holmquist is described by Swedish media as one of the leading figures in the Swedish and international tantra movement.

The Art of Love´s Tantra Therapists.display_name: The Art of Love (tantra therapy collective, founded by Lin Holmquist)
The Art of Love´s Tantra Therapists.bio: The Art of Love is a Swedish tantra therapy school and collective founded by therapist Lin Holmquist, who has worked in therapy for 15+ years (the last decade focused on tantra) and has trained over 100 certified tantra therapists worldwide through her 200-hour Tantra Therapist Training. At Ängsbacka's Tantra Festival, her certified therapists staff several themed 'temple' spaces (Cuddle Temple, Dakini Temple, Temple of Tears) offering guided touch, cuddling, and emotional release/catharsis work to participants.

Theo Cremel.bio: Théo Cremel is an international movement artist and certified Tarraxo teacher with over 14 years of dance experience, blending Hip-Hop, Popping, Breakdance, Capoeira, Salsa, Bachata, Afro, and Kizomba into an expressive style rooted in musicality and grounding. His dance journey began in 2010 in the south of France; in 2018 he left a tech/systems-engineering career to teach dance in China and study naturopathy, yoga, and holistic wellness. He founded Kiz' Island, the first Kizomba festival in Koh Phangan, Thailand.

Tobias.bio: Tobias Lundgren, performing as T.Léon, is a Swedish singer-songwriter and producer with 20+ years in the music industry, having collaborated with artists such as Céline Dion, Chris Stapleton, and Rascal Flatts. He has shifted his focus toward frequency-based, ceremonial music — songs, chants, and guided music journeys drawing on inner exploration, ancestral memory, and the path of healing. He collaborates with Swedish joik artist Jon Henrik Fjällgren and cellist Hale Heikkilä, and serves as Program Coordinator for Ängsbacka's PULSE Music & Dance Festival.

Villu.bio: Villu Kangur offers Conscious Wrestling, a practice grounded in lived experience rather than dogma. His background spans physiotherapy training, Greco-Roman wrestling, Buddhist meditation, tantra, and BDSM exploration, which deepened his respect for consent, surrender, and trust. He describes his facilitation style as grounded, honest, and often a little raw, prioritizing space for laughter, vulnerability, and shadow work.
Villu.location: Estonia (based on name and background; exact city not confirmed)
